creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text:
I suspect that this is just a bug in EDK-II used in qemu 7.2. I suspect if you ran 9.x with the 7.2 efi fw, you'd see the same issue.

And the workaround for it is relatively simple. But we have to go back in time a bit:

commit f89f4898a39f02468a286da27f1dc9fb0b9d5c80
Author: Ed Maste <emaste@FreeBSD.org>
Date:   Mon Apr 15 13:41:53 2019 +0000

    Add quirk for ignoring SPCR AccessWidth values on the PL011 UART

    The SPCR table on the Lenovo HR330A Ampere eMAG server indicates 8-bit
    access, but 32-bit access is required for the PL011 to work.

    PL011 on SBSA platforms always supports 32-bit access (and that was
    hardcoded here before my EC2 fix), let's use 32-bit access for PL011
    and 32BIT interface types.

    Tested by emaste on Ampere eMAG and Cavium/Marvell ThunderX2.

    Submitted by:   Greg V <greg@unrelenting.technology>
    Reviewed by:    andrew, imp (earlier)
    Differential Revision:  https://reviews.freebsd.org/D19507

This commit is basically all wrong. The problem is that we add an ignore bit based on the ACPI info of the device, but we need to ignore the bad data that ACPI is giving to us in the SPCR.

We should throw away that bit. Turns out to not be useful, at all, since firmware upgrades will change, but the device itself will not.

Next, we need to catch the bogus values in uart_dev_pl011 and fix them rather than relying on the string parsing code to do this. My change made us ignore the bit w/o fixing the actual problem, so you hit this bug.

I need more time to do a proper fix, but the heard of the fix is going to be something like

diff --git a/sys/dev/uart/uart_dev_pl011.c b/sys/dev/uart/uart_dev_pl011.c
index 4f946f7c5f36..eaebcb76816d 100644
--- a/sys/dev/uart/uart_dev_pl011.c
+++ b/sys/dev/uart/uart_dev_pl011.c
@@ -172,6 +172,15 @@ static int
 uart_pl011_probe(struct uart_bas *bas)
 {

+ /*
+  * Some SPCR tables have bogus values here. If we detect them here, use
+  * sane defaults. There's no byte-aligned versions of this chip.
+  */
+ if (bas->regshift == 0 && bas->regiowidth == 1) {
+         bas->regshift = 2;
+         bas->regiowidth = 4;
+ }
+
        return (0);
 }

(though I could also fix the boot loader, I haven't decided). I think the above will do the trick for you and if so, I'll start to create a clean chanage.
